steps.title: Translate the first sentence
steps.content:
"Elena has 3 times as many dollars as Noor" means Elena's amount equals 3 times Noor's amount:
$$e=3n$$

steps.title: Translate the second sentence
steps.content:
"Together they have a total of \$48.00" means their combined amount equals 48:
$$e+n=48$$

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $e=3n$ and $e+n=48$: matches both translations. Keep.
  • B) $n=3e$ and $e+n=48$: this would mean Noor has 3 times Elena's money — reversed. Eliminate.
  • C) $e=48n$ and $e+n=3$: wrong multiplier and wrong total. Eliminate.
  • D) $n=48e$ and $e+n=3$: reversed and wrong total. Eliminate.
That confirms A is correct.
